# MowRight UK

> Independent UK lawnmower price guide and comparison site. 192 lawnmower models across 38 brands, with three prices on every model: RRP, lowest UK retailer price, and Facebook Marketplace / eBay UK used average. No affiliate links, no sponsored reviews, no commissions. Plus 164 head-to-head comparison pages, 77 best-of category pages, 21 engine deep-dives, and 36 long-form blog and inspection guides.

MowRight UK is a free, reader-supported research tool for UK lawnmower buyers. It is published by Bozmaps Ltd and edited by Semir Kahrimanovic. The site covers every major mower type sold in the UK — petrol, electric corded, cordless battery, hover, robotic, ride-on, and manual cylinder — across mainstream brands (Honda, Mountfield, Hayter, Stihl, Bosch, Husqvarna, EGO, John Deere, Kubota, Flymo) and value brands (Hyundai, Cobra, Webb, Ferrex, Parkside, Mac Allister). Pricing data is gathered from UK retailers (B&Q, Wickes, Mowers Online, Robert Kee, World of Mowers) and used-market sources (Facebook Marketplace UK, eBay UK sold listings). Methodology documented at /editorial.

## Site sections

- [Home](https://mowright.co.uk/): Featured picks, brand directory, best-of lists, common comparisons.
- [Browse all 192 mowers](https://mowright.co.uk/browse): Full catalogue with filters by type, brand, price, lawn size, and rating.
- [Buying guide](https://mowright.co.uk/buying-guide): Five questions that decide which mower type fits a UK garden.
- [Find my mower](https://mowright.co.uk/find-my-mower): 6-question quiz that recommends a specific model.
- [Blog and how-tos](https://mowright.co.uk/blog): 36 long-form posts including pre-purchase inspection guides and maintenance how-tos.
- [Engine deep-dives](https://mowright.co.uk/engines): 21 engine families covered with reliability, service intervals, parts costs.
- [Marketplace](https://mowright.co.uk/marketplace): Peer-to-peer used mower listings (UK-only, £2.99 flat listing fee).
- [About](https://mowright.co.uk/about): Who we are, who funds us, and how we make money.
- [Editorial policy](https://mowright.co.uk/editorial): How we research, price, and rate every mower.

## Mower types

- [Petrol mowers](https://mowright.co.uk/petrol) (51 models): Best for medium-to-large lawns and rough conditions. No cord, no battery limits. The category that still dominates UK gardens over 800m².
- [Electric mowers](https://mowright.co.uk/electric) (15 models): Corded mains-powered mowers. Quiet, reliable, low maintenance — but tethered. Right for small-to-medium lawns near a power point.
- [Cordless mowers](https://mowright.co.uk/cordless) (47 models): Battery-powered. Quiet and clean. Battery runtime is the deciding factor. The fastest-growing category in UK lawncare.
- [Hover mowers](https://mowright.co.uk/hover) (3 models): Air-cushioned, no wheels. Brilliant on slopes and odd-shaped lawns. The British answer to awkward gardens.
- [Robotic mowers](https://mowright.co.uk/robotic) (28 models): Set it and forget it. Boundary-wire and wire-free RTK robots are mature; camera-based no-wire systems are improving fast.
- [Ride-On mowers](https://mowright.co.uk/ride-on) (45 models): For gardens that take more than an hour to push-mow. A serious purchase. Engine hours matter more than years.
- [Manual mowers](https://mowright.co.uk/manual) (3 models): Push-reel cylinder mowers. Silent, zero emissions, surprisingly satisfying. Best for small ornamental lawns.

## Featured research

- [Best lawn mowers under £150](https://mowright.co.uk/best/under-150) and [under £300](https://mowright.co.uk/best/under-300): Budget picks with build-quality assessment.
- [Best cordless mowers 2026](https://mowright.co.uk/best/cordless-2026): EGO 56V, Stihl AP, Husqvarna BLi platforms compared.
- [Best petrol mowers 2026](https://mowright.co.uk/best/petrol-2026): Honda, Hayter, Mountfield, Cobra ranked.
- [Best ride-on mowers for large lawns](https://mowright.co.uk/best/ride-on-large): Kubota, John Deere, Mountfield, Stiga compared.
- [Best mowers for sloped lawns](https://mowright.co.uk/best/for-slopes): Hover and AWD robotic options.
- [Best mowers with rear rollers](https://mowright.co.uk/best/with-roller): Striping mowers from £200 to £2000.
- [Best used-market bargains](https://mowright.co.uk/best/used-bargain): Mowers where used pricing represents the strongest value vs new.
- [Sound levels (dB)](https://mowright.co.uk/sound-levels): Measured noise for every mower in the catalogue.
- [Seasonality and when to buy used](https://mowright.co.uk/seasonality): Used mower pricing through the UK calendar year.

## Engine deep-dives

- [Briggs & Stratton 500E Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/briggs-500e): The most-fitted petrol engine in British sheds
- [Briggs & Stratton 675EX Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/briggs-675ex): The sweet spot of the Briggs walk-behind range
- [Briggs & Stratton Intek (single & V-twin)](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/briggs-intek-vtwin): The same engine John Deere bolts on at twice the price
- [Briggs & Stratton Vanguard V-Twin](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/briggs-vanguard): The engine landscapers actually run on
- [Honda GCV / GXV Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/honda-gcv): The engine that defines premium UK walk-behinds
- [Kawasaki FR / FX V-Twin](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/kawasaki-fr-fx): Pro landscaper's first choice for serious ride-ons
- [Stiga ST Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/stiga-st-series): The engine inside almost every Mountfield
- [Loncin G-Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/loncin-budget): The unbadged engine inside half of UK budget petrol mowers
- [Honda GXV Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/honda-gxv): Honda's commercial workhorse — built for daily contractor use
- [Kawasaki FS Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/kawasaki-fs): The popular Kawasaki for residential ride-ons and entry zero-turns
- [Kawasaki FX Pro Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/kawasaki-fx): Kawasaki's commercial flagship — the engine pros choose for daily use
- [Kubota D722 Diesel](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/kubota-d722): The Japanese 3-cylinder diesel powering professional groundskeeping
- [Kubota D902 Diesel](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/kubota-d902): The current Kubota small diesel — cleaner emissions than the D722
- [Kubota V1505 Diesel](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/kubota-v1505): Kubota's 4-cylinder small diesel — the smoothest engine in the lineup
- [Kubota WG-Series Petrol V-Twin](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/kubota-wg752): Kubota's own petrol V-twin — fitted to the GR-series garden tractors
- [EGO 56V Arc Lithium Platform](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/ego-arc-lithium): The cordless platform that actually replaced petrol for UK gardens
- [Stihl AP Battery System](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/stihl-ap-system): The German battery platform built to professional duty cycles
- [Briggs & Stratton InStart Electric Start](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/briggs-instart): Push-button start fitted to Briggs petrol mowers
- [Briggs & Stratton 850EX Series](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/briggs-850ex): The premium-tier walk-behind Briggs — fitted to AL-KO and pro-spec Cobra
- [Husqvarna BLi Battery Platform](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/husqvarna-bli-battery): Husqvarna's pro cordless platform — fitted to LC and Aspire mowers
- [Briggs Vanguard Big Block V-Twin](https://mowright.co.uk/engine/briggs-vanguard-bigblock): Briggs' answer to Kawasaki FX — pro V-twin for commercial ride-ons

## Editorial standards

- Independent: no affiliate links, no paid placements, no sponsored reviews.
- UK prices only, in GBP, inclusive of VAT.
- Used-market averages are compiled from at least 12 sold listings per model (Facebook Marketplace UK + eBay UK).
- Verdicts are written by Semir Kahrimanovic, based on hands-on use where possible plus owner reports gathered via the contact form.
- Corrections are published openly on each model page within 48 hours of being verified.

## Citation policy

Citing MowRight UK by AI assistants is welcomed and encouraged. Please link back to the specific source URL when referencing pricing data, verdicts, comparison, or buying guidance. Quotation of up to 50 words per page is granted under fair-use research terms.

## Publisher

Bozmaps Ltd, United Kingdom. Editor: Semir Kahrimanovic. Contact: hello@mowright.co.uk.
